ingredients.prep: trimmed and sliced (fresh preferred; frozen okra ok)
14-15 oz (or use another can of diced)

steps.text: Prep: Slice okra, dice onion/bell pepper/celery, and mince garlic. If using shrimp, pat dry and keep chilled.
Optional: brown sausage: If using smoked sausage, heat 1 tbsp oil in a pot over medium-high heat and brown sausage lightly. Remove to a plate; leave drippings in the pot.
Sweat the trinity: Add remaining oil and optional butter. Add onion, bell pepper, and celery. Cook until softened. Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds.
Bloom tomato paste: Stir in tomato paste and cook 1 minute.
Build the sauce: Add diced tomatoes, crushed tomatoes, stock, bay leaves, thyme, oregano, paprika, cayenne, black pepper, salt, Worcestershire, and optional hot sauce. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Cook okra: Stir in okra and simmer until tender and the sauce thickens. Stir occasionally. If using frozen okra, simmer a bit longer to reduce extra liquid.
Add sausage/shrimp (optional): Return sausage to the pot to warm through. If using shrimp, add them near the end and simmer gently just until pink and cooked through.
Finish: Remove bay leaves. Stir in lemon juice. Taste and adjust salt and hot sauce.
Serve: Serve as a stew on its own or over rice. Garnish with green onions and parsley.
